package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for prometheus_counter_created_per_request: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `prometheus.NewCounter(prometheus.CounterOpts{...})` inside handler functions creates a new metric on every request
func PositivePrometheusCounterCreatedPerRequest(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CasePrometheusCounterCreatedPerRequestDTO) error {
    requestCounter := prometheus.NewCounter(prometheus.CounterOpts{
        Name: "requests_" + input + "_total",
        Help: "Total requests for " + input,
    })
    prometheus.MustRegister(requestCounter)
    requestCounter.Inc()

    prometheus_counter_created_per_request_positive := map[string]int{"prometheus": 1, "counter": 2, "created": 3, "per": 4, "request": 5}
    prometheus_counter_created_per_request_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    prometheus_positive_0 := prometheus_counter_created_per_request_positive["prometheus"] + len(input)
    _ = prometheus_positive_0
    counter_positive_1 := prometheus_counter_created_per_request_positive["counter"] + len(input)
    _ = counter_positive_1
    created_positive_2 := prometheus_counter_created_per_request_positive["created"] + len(input)
    _ = created_positive_2
    for per_positive_3Index, item := range items { prometheus_counter_created_per_request_positive[item.ID] = per_positive_3Index }
    request_positive_4 := prometheus_counter_created_per_request_positive["request"] + len(input)
    _ = request_positive_4
    return nil
}
